edge_hypothesis: Fee drag is the dominant cost: 62% of gross profit on ETH/4h at 389 trades. The core question is whether a SIMPLER signal with FEWER trades preserves the trend edge while eliminating fee erosion. EMA_9/EMA_21 crossovers fire only on actual trend changes (est. 30-60 trades vs 389), producing structurally fewer entries. By stripping away RSI confirmation and ATR volatility gates, we isolate the base trend signal. If EMA crossover alone shows PF > 1.0 on fewer trades, the ema_rsi_atr edge is real but hidden by overtrading. If PF < 0.9, the confirmation layers are necessary and the edge is in entry selectivity, not trend direction.
regime_assumption: TRENDING. EMA crossovers are pure trend signals — they perform when price sustains directional movement through both EMAs. ADX > 20 gate ensures minimum trend strength. Expected to produce very few trades in ranging markets naturally (crossovers are rare without trend). Transitional regimes are the risk: whipsaw crossovers during trend shifts. Invalidate if total trades < 20 (insufficient sample) or trending PF < 0.90.
invalidation_conditions: Total trades < 20 (insufficient sample for EMA crossover frequency)
Fee drag still exceeds 50% of gross profit despite reduced frequency
Win rate below 30% (crossover timing too poor even with wide stops)

variants.name: ema_cross_low_freq_wide
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover with very wide exits. Core test: does fewer trades + wider stops preserve trend edge while slashing fee drag? Targets ~30-60 trades vs pipeline's 389.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (golden cross on fast EMAs).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (death cross on fast EMAs).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: Inherent — EMA crossovers naturally filter to trending regimes
exit_rules: Stop loss at 3.0x ATR below entry (wide: let trend develop through noise).
Take profit at 5.0x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.67).
Exit on opposite EMA crossover signal.
Exit on max 40 bars in trade (6.7 days on 4h — hold through full trend leg).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Whipsaw EMA crosses in ranging consolidation
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Wide stop hit before trend develops
risk_rules: Wide stops require position sizing discipline — 3.0 ATR stop means smaller position size.

components.notes: EMA_9 and EMA_21 crossover as sole entry signal
No separate confirmation — crossover IS the signal

variants.name: ema_cross_moderate_exit
variants.description: EMA crossover with moderate exits (2.0/3.5 ATR). Tests whether tighter exits improve PF by cutting losers faster, or whether the wider variant's patience is needed for EMA signals.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR below entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.75).
Exit on opposite EMA crossover signal.
Exit on max 25 bars in trade.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Tighter stop clips winners prematurely
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Crossover lag means entry is already mid-move

components.notes: Crossover is the signal — no separate confirmation

variants.name: ema_cross_1h_scalp
variants.description: EMA crossover on ETH/1h with tight exits. Tests whether higher-frequency crossovers can exploit the ETH/1h ranging PF=1.115 finding — crossovers in ranges are mean-reversion signals rather than trend entries.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR below entry (tight: quick mean-reversion trades).
Take profit at 2.0x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.33).
Exit on opposite EMA crossover signal.
Exit on max 12 bars in trade (12h hold max).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Fee erosion on high 1h trade frequency
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Crossover whipsaw in consolidation
risk_rules: Reduced risk for 1h frequency — fee sensitivity is high.
